summary: Iteration 14 (auto-notify on order/shipment state transitions) fully verified. Wrote 13-case pytest suite covering resolve_preset_tokens unit tests (5 cases: line ctx, shipment ctx, no ctx fallback, missing-token dash, unknown preset), line qty_status → in_production/ready auto-fire (2), idempotence on same-status PATCH (1), line ETA change → schedule_revision (1), shipment dispatch → shipment_dispatched (1), shipment deliver → shipment_delivered (1), manual /notify regression (1), best-effort 200-response contract (1). All 13 pass. Ran full regression iter9+iter10+iter11+iter12+iter13 = 73/73 pass. To avoid hitting real customer numbers I temporarily set RESTRICT_OUTBOUND_TO_PHONE=+918200663263 and RESTRICT_OUTBOUND_TO_EMAIL=qa@hre.test in backend/.env, restarted backend, ran tests, then restored .env to blank and restarted (verified /api/health/integrations returns restrict_outbound_phone='' post-teardown).

minor.issue: Dead-code block: `if data.qty_status is not None and li.get('qty_status') != (items[line_idx].get('qty_status') if False else data.qty_status): pass`. The `if False else …` collapses to `data.qty_status`, comparing `data.qty_status != data.qty_status` → always False. The block is a no-op. The comment on line 204 acknowledges the real gate is the `changes` list a few lines below. Recommend deleting lines 203-205 for readability.

`from services.universal_update import auto_send_preset` is imported inside the function on every PATCH. Fine functionally but a top-of-file import would be marginally cleaner and matches the existing pattern for /notify which also does an inline import (also worth hoisting).

action_items: OPTIONAL: remove dead-code block at orders.py:203-205 (see minor issue).
critical_code_review_comments: auto_send_preset correctly swallows all exceptions with logger.exception — verified best-effort contract via successful 200 return path.
resolve_preset_tokens context precedence is correct: line ETA overrides order.expected_completion_date; shipment transporter overrides order.dispatch.transporter_name.

Idempotence is enforced by gating auto-notify on `changes` list content ('status:' or 'ETA:' prefix) rather than on the raw PATCH body — a same-value PATCH produces empty `changes` and is correctly skipped.
Auto-notify entries carry kind='auto_universal_update' (vs manual kind='universal_update') so admins can filter/distinguish in the timeline — verified in both manual and auto paths.
Shipment auto-notify persists shipment_id and shipment_number into the notification entry for admin auditability — nice touch.
updated_files: /app/backend/.env (temp: set RESTRICT_OUTBOUND_TO_PHONE for test run then restored to blank — final state matches pre-test state)
seed_data_creation: None — used existing orders in DB. Test creates its own shipment on a free line item and uploads dummy PDF docs for the dispatch gate.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 14 auto-notify pipeline is verified end-to-end. When testing anything that touches services/universal_update.send_universal_update, always temporarily set RESTRICT_OUTBOUND_TO_PHONE in backend/.env + restart to reroute real WhatsApp sends to Harsh's QA phone (+918200663263), then restore to blank + restart after tests. iter10 test_settings_integrations_exposes_test_mode expects RESTRICT_OUTBOUND_TO_PHONE=+918200663263 so if that env is intentionally blank the assertion is looser (accepts either). Regression suite is stable at 73 pass.
rca of the issue: n/a — this was a feature verification (auto-notify wiring), not a bug fix.
